title: phalangium longiscapum
intro: Phalangium longiscapum, also known as False Nettle or False Cleavers, is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its climbing habit and small, white flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phalangium longiscapum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and colds, and provide relief from skin irritations and insect bites. Phalangium longiscapum can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
